scope: Verify whether Ralph docs watchdog job c99cfa5f-41a1-4bc2-845f-6b6e1d5c7e80 is actually executing every 10 minutes; inspect cron state, run history, running state, status artifacts, and blockers; repair locally if safe.
findings: User crontab contains only comments for the Ralph docs watchdog runner/verifier; there is no actual cron command installed for either job.
cron.service is active, but there is no evidence of scheduled executions for the docs watchdog path in crontab or accessible system cron fragments.

The watchdog/log artifacts are stale and inconsistent: watchdog_cron.log last updated 2026-05-20 19:02:11 +0200, verifier_cron.log 2026-05-20 19:11:38 +0200, while current status/signoff files changed later via manual/subagent activity.
Manual execution of docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py did not prove health. It failed with 'verifier failed' and 'parallel signoff fingerprint does not match current docs state'.

A live Ralph docs loop/runner process was found running, which caused ralph_docs_verify.py to exit 75 with 'another Ralph docs loop process already holds the global lock'. That means current execution is lock-contented and not a clean scheduled heartbeat.
The watchdog execution path is therefore not proven healthy every 10 minutes; at best it is being invoked ad hoc/manually and can block on concurrent loop activity.
repairsApplied: No persistent scheduler repair applied, because installing or changing cron entries would alter persistent scheduling state and was not safe to do blindly from a verifier role.
Killed stray overlapping docs watchdog/verify/runner processes during verification to confirm lock contention source and avoid leaving extra verifier noise behind.
proof: crontab -l shows lines 15-16 are only comments: '# Ralph docs watchdog runner' and '# Ralph docs independent verifier' with no commands beneath them.
ps showed concurrent docs processes including ralph_docs_runner.py and ralph_docs_agentic_review.py, and later ralph_docs_verify.py holding the global lock path agents/docs_quality/.docs_loop.lock.

agents/docs_quality/docs_stack_temp_watchdog_status.json at 2026-05-20 20:44:18 UTC reported healthy=false with reasons ['verifier failed', 'parallel signoff fingerprint does not match current docs state'].
agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_verify.py can emit exit 75 on lock contention, which was observed in the watchdog run output as 'SKIP: another Ralph docs loop process already holds the global lock'.
Current signoff fingerprint and docs-state fingerprint diverged (signoff a0b7bc42142e43c1 vs watchdog fa50764b881e95d0), so even the deactivation proof was stale for the present docs state.
blockers: Missing actual 10-minute cron entries for the watchdog/verifier jobs.
Concurrent docs loop activity causes verifier lock contention and prevents a clean health signal.
Because scheduling is absent, I cannot truthfully certify real every-10-minute execution behavior.
